version.description: Version of the .skilled_crew.yaml schema format. Currently `"1.0"`. Bumped when the schema gains breaking changes.

id.description: Stable identifier for this skillet. Used in session paths, cache keys, and cost tracking, so it should be unique across skillets you run on the same machine.

commands.description: Slash commands the user can invoke during chat (e.g. typing `/french hello` triggers the `french` command). Each command maps to a `.command.md` file.

name.description: Name of the slash command. The user invokes it during chat by typing `/<name> [arguments]`. Must be unique within this skillet.

filePath.description: Path to the `.command.md` file containing the command instructions. Relative to the .skilled_crew.yaml file. `$ARGUMENTS` inside the file is substituted with the text the user typed after the command name.

items.description: Definition of a single slash command available to the user during chat.

entryPointAgent.description: Name of the agent that receives the user's messages first when multiple agents are defined. Must match a key in `agents`. If null and there are multiple agents, the first one wins; ignored when there is only one agent.

agents.description: Map of agent name → agent definition. Each key is the agent's name (referenced by `entryPointAgent`). The simplest skillets define one agent.

instructionsPath.description: Path to the AGENTS.md instructions file for this agent. Relative to the .skilled_crew.yaml file. If null, the agent runs with no extra instructions beyond its skills.

codeWorkerPath.description: Path to a deterministic code-worker module (.ts/.js), relative to the .skilled_crew.yaml file. When set, jobs assigned to this name run that module's exported entry function as plain code instead of instantiating an LLM agent — no model is ever created. Mutually exclusive with `instructionsPath`/`skills`. Use for steps whose logic is fully deterministic (e.g. an approval gate driving an irreversible action).

mcp_servers.description: MCP servers exposed as tools to this agent. Each entry points to a JSON config file describing how to launch the server.

filePath.description: Path to a JSON file describing an MCP (Model Context Protocol) server (command, args, env). Relative to the .skilled_crew.yaml file. The agent gets the MCP server's tools.

items.description: Reference to an MCP server config file. The agent that lists it gets the server's tools.

skills.description: Skills available to this agent. Each skill becomes a callable sub-agent the orchestrator can hand work off to.

name.description: Skill name. Used by the orchestrator to route user requests to this skill. Should match the folder name and the `name` in the skill's SKILL.md frontmatter.

folderPath.description: Path to the skill folder. Relative to the .skilled_crew.yaml file. The folder must contain a SKILL.md (frontmatter + instructions) and may contain executable scripts that the skill can call.

items.description: Reference to a skill folder. Each skill becomes a tool-calling sub-agent that the orchestrator can route to.

additionalProperties.description: Definition of one agent inside this skillet. The map key (e.g. `todo_list_agent`) is the agent name used when referring to it as `entryPointAgent` or in routing.

script_runtime.description: Configures where and how skill scripts run. Defaults to `kind: local` (host child processes).

kind.description: How skill scripts execute. `local`: run as child processes on the host (default, no isolation). `container`: build the Docker image from `dockerfile` and run scripts inside it (isolation + reproducible deps).

dockerfile.description: Path to a Dockerfile, relative to the .skilled_crew.yaml file. Required when `kind` is `container`; ignored when `kind` is `local`.

description: Configuration for a skillet (an AI agent setup driven by markdown). Loaded from a `.skilled_crew.yaml` file by the skillet_agent CLI.
